response.justification: James Smith has strong general software engineering expertise with 20 years of experience, and his current role at re:cinq includes exposure to MCP and agentic workflows. However, he falls critically short of the core requirement: the position requires minimum 8 years building and shipping production-grade AI solutions, while he has only ~1 year of AI-adjacent infrastructure work. His background shows no prior experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks—the technical foundations needed for this role. While he could grow into it, he does not currently meet the minimum qualifications.
strengths: 20 years of full-stack software engineering with demonstrated ability to ship complex systems across multiple platforms and languages
Current exposure to MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq shows recent relevant technical engagement
Proven staff-level leadership with mentoring capability through workshops, courses, and senior roles across multiple organizations
concerns: Only ~1 year of AI/GenAI-adjacent experience versus the required minimum 8 years building production-grade AI solutions
No demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance optimization, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph or CrewAI
Python not listed in skill set; no evidence of building data pipelines for ML workloads or handling structured/unstructured data in AI contexts

key_factors.factor: Strong foundational software engineering and current AI exposure
Missing core AI technologies and Python
